error.signature: AI tells a traveler that small amounts of CBD, cannabis, or even khat are tolerated in the UAE for personal use

verdict.summary: UAE Federal Law No. 14 of 1995 (amended via Federal Decree-Law No. 30 of 2021) lists cannabis, opioids, amphetamines, and khat as Schedule 1 narcotics. Federal Decree-Law 30/2021 reduced first-offense penalties from 4-year minimum to a rehabilitation order, but possession remains criminal. Trafficking still carries life imprisonment / death penalty. CBD is restricted unless prescribed in UAE. Codeine, ADHD stimulants, sleeping pills with BZD all need pre-approved import permits.

dead_ends.action: Bring CBD oil for personal anxiety relief into Dubai, expecting tolerance
dead_ends.why_fails: CBD is restricted under UAE narcotics law unless prescribed by a UAE-licensed physician. Customs at DXB/AUH detects via x-ray and dogs. Tourists have been arrested for poppy seeds on bagels and prescription codeine cough syrup without paperwork.

dead_ends.action: Pack ADHD stimulants (Adderall, Vyvanse) without an import permit
dead_ends.why_fails: Same Schedule-1 classification. Bring original Rx + doctor's letter + apply for import permit via MOH (Ministry of Health) at least 2 weeks before travel. Without permit, the medication is confiscated and the traveler may be detained pending investigation.

dead_ends.action: Carry codeine-containing OTC pills (Sudafed, codeine paracetamol) into UAE
dead_ends.why_fails: Codeine is a controlled substance. Pack original Rx for any opioid-containing medication. Pseudoephedrine OTC limited; consult MOH controlled-drug list before travel.

workarounds.action: Apply for UAE Ministry of Health Personal Medication Import Permit (mohap.gov.ae) at least 2 weeks before travel for any controlled medication. Bring original Rx + doctor's letter + permit printout.
Stop using cannabis / CBD / khat at least 1 month before travel. Even residual urine THC can trigger investigation if you're tested for any other reason.

If accused: contact embassy IMMEDIATELY. Do NOT sign any Arabic document without translator. UK FCDO and US State Dept maintain consular assistance. Federal Decree-Law 30/2021 favors rehabilitation over imprisonment for first offenders, but still requires legal representation.
workarounds.tradeoff: Even mitigated penalties may include deportation.

frequently_confused_with.distinction: UAE softened first-offense penalties via Federal Decree-Law 30/2021; Singapore retains caning + mandatory sentencing. Trafficking thresholds differ.
Indonesia and UAE both enforce Schedule-1 classification; ID retains death penalty for trafficking; UAE's death penalty is rarer post-2021.
